topic: EXP-249 — Phase B1 pie chart cleanup (token-route fills, --label-* ink for AA, larger legend stride, drop matchMedia)
our_argument_summary: EXP-249 is the first of four commissioned Phase B EXPs (HNOTE-PHASE-B-DESIGN-REFRESH-COMMISSION-001 §B1). Six coordinated changes in build-scripts/generate-html.js: (1) append six --label-{refuted,stdmodel,selfcon,misleading,notdemo,unfalsifiable} hex tokens to :root light at line 496, (2) append the dark-theme overrides to the @media(prefers-color-scheme:dark):root at line 497, (3) append the same light values to @media print:root at line 645/646, (4) replace VERDICT_COLORS_LIGHT and VERDICT_COLORS_DARK plus the entire generatePieChart body (~lines 315-432) with a token-driven version that emits fill=var(--*-solid) / fill=var(--label-*) plus a larger legend (24x24 swatch, 48px row stride, 320 wide), (5) delete the matchMedia DOM-toggle script appended after the chart, (6) leave the verdict bar chart, table cells, kill-shot badges, sections.json, and wins.json untouched (per HNOTE boundary). Function signature, returned HTML envelope, class names, ARIA labels, slice ordering, geometry, and SMALL_THRESHOLD are all preserved.
proposal_review_lens: DIRECTIVE-003 design rubric (used for EXP-211-redesign series). Lens: independently re-derive contrast claims, validate line-number anchors against current source, audit structural preservation, attack acceptance-criteria precision, surface visual deltas the EXP omits.

relevant_files: build-scripts/generate-html.js (lines 315-432 — pie chart code; lines 496/497/646 — :root token blocks; lines 540-572 — existing var(--*-solid) consumers)
docs/index.html (rendered output — current pie chart at the WINS tab Part 3.1)
test.js (no pie internals asserted; legend-count contract is loose substring match)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No data pipeline change. tally and total are still passed as args; substitution into HTML/SVG is unchanged. WCAG ratios stated in acceptance_criteria.contrast_table are pure pre-computed reference numbers — not used at render time.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: EXP correctly notes that the new --label-* tokens are hex literals (not aliases of --ink/--bg). Rationale (anticipated_objections[2]) is sound: --ink/--bg are tuned for body text, not against verdict-tinted backgrounds, and literal hex makes the audit trail one-step.
data_sources_used: tally object (computed upstream from data/wins.json verdicts)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Two soft hedges: (a) acceptance_criteria.contrast_table flags SelfCon-dark 4.3:1 as borderline and offers a 16->18px font-size bump as a one-line follow-up; (b) anticipated_objections[6] notes that the legend AAA tap-target framing assumes a future B+002 EXP wraps each row in <a>. Both hedges are reasonable but both interact with hole H3 (see below) — the spec presents the AAA improvement as already-realized rather than conditional.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: Two anchor inaccuracies: (a) change_3.where cites '@media print:root declaration around line 645'. Actual line is 646 — the @media print rule scoping :root opens at line 646. Off by one. (b) anticipated_objections[1] cites the existing var() pattern as 'line 547 (.vb-bar.vb-refuted{background:var(--refuted-solid)})'. Actual line is 548. Both are minor documentation drift, not structural defects.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Not applicable — project-internal HTML/CSS spec, not a dome-model claim review.

contrast_audit.method: Independently recomputed each contrast_table claim using the WCAG 2.1 relative-luminance formula (sRGB linearization piecewise; L = 0.2126*R + 0.7152*G + 0.0722*B; ratio = (L_lighter+0.05)/(L_darker+0.05)). Token values for --refuted-solid, --semantic-crit, and the *-solid family read directly from generate-html.js lines 496, 497, 646.
contrast_audit.summary: Every claim passes WCAG AA when independently computed. Direction of finding is correct (current chart fails AA on white-on-Misleading-orange ~1.94:1 and white-on-Unfalsif-gray ~1.88:1; after fix, all twelve combos clear 4.5:1). However, several stated ratios diverge meaningfully from the recomputed values, which undercuts the EXP framing of the table as 'auditable arithmetic, not vibes'. Most consequential: Misleading-light + dark ink claimed 12:1, recomputed ~8.96:1 (off by ~3.0). Most operationally relevant: Unfalsifiable-dark + light ink claimed 5.5:1, recomputed ~4.61:1 — that is the genuinely tightest dark-mode case, not SelfCon-dark as the EXP flags.

Misleading light (#FFA726 on #1a1a1a).note: Largest discrepancy. Still passes AAA, but the audit is overstated.

SelfCon dark (#1e88e5 on #1a1a1a).note: EXP flags this as borderline; recomputation shows it actually clears 4.5:1 by 0.23.

Unfalsif dark (#757575 on #fff).note: Genuinely tightest dark-mode case. Only 0.11 above the 4.5 threshold. Any future shift of #757575 toward midtone could break AA.

visual_delta_audit.summary: EXP acceptance_criteria.visual contradicts EXP counter_considerations on the most visible change in light theme. The Refuted slice is currently filled with hex literal #E57373 (read directly from VERDICT_COLORS_LIGHT line 319 of generate-html.js). After the change, fill resolves to var(--refuted-solid) which equals var(--semantic-crit) = #7a2e2e in light mode. That is a substantial darkening of the largest red block on the page, not 'no shift'. The EXP itself documents the shift correctly in counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_address[2] ('the slice itself becomes the deeper red... worth flagging so it is not mistaken for a regression'), but acceptance_criteria.visual[a] says: 'Refuted slice ink stays white but the slice itself was already on var(--refuted-solid) per EXP-210; no shift.' The 'already on var(--refuted-solid) per EXP-210' clause is incorrect — EXP-210 routed table cells, bar chart, kill-shot badges, and verdict-tag chips through tokens, but explicitly LEFT the pie chart on its own hex map (the EXP itself acknowledges this in its own kernel_of_truth and in the change_4 rationale). The contradiction is internal and consequential because acceptance_criteria.visual is what an integration tester checks.
secondary_visual_deltas_not_mentioned: Callout-line stroke and text color (small slices, fraction < 8%) currently hardcode darkColor in BOTH themes — the matchMedia toggle does not touch .callout-line / .pie-callout. After the change, callouts use var(--*-solid) which IS theme-responsive. So in LIGHT theme, callouts shift from the bright dark-theme hex (e.g. #ef5350 for Refuted) to the deep light-theme value (e.g. #7a2e2e). For Not Demonstrated and Unfalsifiable (both at 4 wins, both below the 8% threshold and therefore drawn with callouts), light-mode callouts get noticeably darker. This is arguably an improvement (consistency with theme), but the EXP visual list does not enumerate it.

In-slice labels for Std Model / Self-Contradicted go from white-with-text-shadow to dark ink with no shadow. EXP visual[b] mentions this at a high level but not the loss of the text-shadow style attribute, which had been doing visual lift on small in-slice labels at the slice edge.

The pie chart currently emits 6 .pie-slice paths (or fewer if any verdict tally is zero). Today's tally has all six populated. Geometry and ordering are preserved, so this is not a hole — just confirming.

legend_tap_target_audit.finding: acceptance_criteria.visual[c] claims 'tap target envelope now satisfies WCAG 2.5.5 AAA (44x44)'. The legend rows are currently <g> elements containing a <rect> swatch and a <text> label with no enclosing <a>, no click handler, and no rect background spanning the row. There is no tap target. WCAG 2.5.5 (Target Size) applies to interactive controls; for non-interactive content the criterion does not apply. The EXP correctly recognizes this in anticipated_objections[6] ('the legend is not currently a click target') but the acceptance criterion overstates: the chart does not 'satisfy' AAA tap-target sizing because there is no target to size.
legend_tap_target_audit.honest_framing: The legend swatch enlargement and 48px row stride are forward-compat sizing for HNOTE-PHASE-B-VERDICT-CHIP-DEFINITION-LINK-002 (currently delivered as EXP-252, in queue but not in this batch) — when each row is wrapped in <a>, the row-stride will absorb taps without further reflow. That is a defensible design decision but the criterion should read something like: 'Legend rows sized so that, when made interactive in a subsequent EXP, they will satisfy WCAG 2.5.5 AAA (44x44) without further reflow.'

test_impact.test_js_runs_now: PASS — node test.js against current main reports 3452 passed, 0 failed. EXP claim that tests still pass is plausible because: legend-count contract uses a substring match, and the surrounding prose elsewhere in the page satisfies the OR-of-candidates assertion. Pie/legend internals are not asserted.
test_impact.untested_acceptance_criteria: acceptance_criteria.code item 3 ('grep matchMedia inside pie chart block returns zero') and item 4 ('grep hex literals inside generatePieChart returns zero matches') are NOT enforced by test.js. They are integration-time spot-checks. EXP does not propose new test.js assertions for them, which means a regression that re-introduces a hex literal or a JS toggle would not be caught by automated tests. Recommend the decider add lightweight tests at integration time.

scope_and_boundary_audit.boundary_respected: Yes. Verdict bar chart (.vb-*), table verdict cells (td.v-*), kill-shot badges (.ks-status), sections.json, and wins.json are all correctly out of scope. CSS rules outside the three :root blocks are untouched. The change does not introduce new component prefixes (which would be HNOTE §B3 / EXP-251 territory). VERDICT_ORDER preserved.
scope_and_boundary_audit.naming_convention: Six new tokens use the pattern --label-{verdict-slug}. The existing verdict family uses --refuted, --stdmodel, --selfcon, --misleading, --unfalsifiable, --notdemo (and *-solid siblings). The new tokens grep-align well ('--label-refuted' is consistent with '--refuted-solid'). One nit: the legacy family uses --notdemo (no hyphen between 'not' and 'demo'), and the EXP follows suit with --label-notdemo. Internally consistent. Acceptable.
scope_and_boundary_audit.forward_compat: anticipated_objections[2] correctly notes that --label-* could be re-aliased to a future semantic-ink palette without breaking the call sites. The chart 'var(--label-refuted)' reference remains valid through any palette evolution as long as the token name persists.

kernel_of_truth.description: The proposal is fundamentally sound. Token routing for slice fills is the correct fix and brings the pie chart into line with the rest of the design system established in EXP-209/210. The current WCAG AA failures (white-on-orange ~1.94:1 and white-on-gray ~1.88:1) are real and the proposed per-verdict ink choice clears 4.5:1 in every combo. Removing the matchMedia DOM-toggle is genuinely better engineering: CSS @media handles theme switching natively, eliminates a JS race condition on first paint, and makes print rendering deterministic without JS. Function signature, returned HTML envelope, class names, ARIA labels, geometry, and SMALL_THRESHOLD are all preserved verbatim — the structural preservation discipline is excellent. The forward-compat sizing of the legend stride for HNOTE-002 is good engineering hygiene. The proposal is conservative, well-scoped, and ready for integration with the documentation-quality fixes flagged below.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Not applicable — this is an internal proposal review, not a dome-model claim review. The kernel here is acknowledging that EXP-249 is substantively sound; the holes below are documentation precision, not structural defects.

holes_found.description: acceptance_criteria.visual[a] is internally contradictory and factually incorrect on the most visible light-theme change. It claims 'Refuted slice ink stays white but the slice itself was already on var(--refuted-solid) per EXP-210; no shift'. This is wrong on two counts: (1) the pie chart was NOT routed through --refuted-solid by EXP-210 — the EXP itself acknowledges this in its main rationale and in change_4 ('every other verdict-encoded surface on the page now resolves through --refuted-solid... but the pie chart still owns its own hex literals'), and (2) counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_address[2] correctly states that the Refuted slice DOES shift visually from #E57373 to #7a2e2e (the deeper light-theme red). The acceptance criterion is the integration tester's checklist — telling them 'no shift' and then having them see a substantially darker red slice creates a false-positive regression report.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite acceptance_criteria.visual[a] to: 'Light-theme Refuted slice darkens from #E57373 to var(--refuted-solid) = #7a2e2e — visually unifies with the verdict bar chart and table cells (which were routed through --refuted-solid in EXP-210). Slice ink remains white. This is intentional, not a regression.' Keep counter_considerations[2] as-is; it has the right framing already.

holes_found.description: Contrast table arithmetic does not reproduce. Independent recomputation of each of the 12 stated ratios using the WCAG 2.1 formula yields deltas ranging from -3.04 (Misleading light, claimed 12:1, actual 8.96:1) to +0.84 (Misleading dark, claimed 6.5:1, actual 7.34:1). Eight of twelve are off by 0.4 or more. Direction is correct in every case (all combos pass AA), but the EXP frames the table as 'auditable arithmetic, not vibes' and the arithmetic does not audit. Most operationally important: SelfCon dark is flagged as the 'borderline AA' case (claimed 4.3:1) — recomputation gives 4.73:1 (clears AA cleanly). The genuinely tightest dark case is Unfalsifiable dark (claimed 5.5:1, actual 4.61:1, only 0.11 above threshold). If a future palette tweak shifts #757575 toward midtone gray, AA could break — and the EXP currently advises font-size mitigation for the wrong slice.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace the contrast_table with recomputed values (use the WCAG 2.1 luminance formula). Re-flag the borderline case to Unfalsifiable-dark, not SelfCon-dark. The font-size fallback (16->18px to qualify for 'large text' 3:1 threshold) still applies but should be motivated by the correct slice. Recomputed values are listed in this review's contrast_audit.recomputed_table — copy them across.

holes_found.description: acceptance_criteria.visual[c] claims the new legend 'tap target envelope now satisfies WCAG 2.5.5 AAA (44x44)'. This overstates the immediate gain. The legend rows are <g> elements containing a non-interactive <rect> swatch and a non-interactive <text> label. There is no enclosing <a>, no click/touch handler, no row-spanning background rect, and no other pointer-event surface. WCAG 2.5.5 applies to interactive controls; with no target, the criterion does not apply. The 48px row stride is genuinely useful as forward-compat sizing for HNOTE-002 / EXP-252 (verdict-chip click-through), but the proposal as a SELF-CONTAINED unit does not deliver an AAA tap target — it only sizes the layout so that B+002 will. The acceptance criterion currently reads as if the AAA target ships in B1.
holes_found.recommendation: Reword acceptance_criteria.visual[c] to: 'Legend swatch enlarged from 16x16 to 24x24 and row stride from 28px to 48px. The 48px stride is sized so that, once HNOTE-PHASE-B-VERDICT-CHIP-DEFINITION-LINK-002 wraps each legend row in <a>, the row will satisfy WCAG 2.5.5 AAA (44x44) without further reflow. This EXP delivers the sizing; AAA conformance lands when the click-through ships.' That keeps the design decision honest.

holes_found.description: Line-number drift in two spots: (a) change_3.where references '@media print:root declaration around line 645' — actual line is 646. (b) anticipated_objections[1] cites 'line 547 (.vb-bar.vb-refuted{background:var(--refuted-solid)})' — actual line is 548. Off-by-one in both. The structural anchors are unambiguous from context (the @media print rule is unique; the .vb-bar.vb-refuted selector is unique), so the line numbers do not drive integration. But the same kind of drift in EXP-211-redesign-v2 generated a finding, so flagging here for consistency.
holes_found.recommendation: Either correct the line numbers (645->646, 547->548) or describe positions structurally ('the @media print:root rule, currently the only @media print scoping :root in the file').

holes_found.description: acceptance_criteria.code item 3 ('Searching the rendered docs/index.html for matchMedia inside the pie chart block returns zero hits') and item 4 ('Searching for hex color literals inside generatePieChart returns zero matches') are aspirational acceptance criteria. test.js does not enforce them. After integration, a regression that re-introduces a hex literal (e.g., a future maintainer adding a slice highlight) or re-introduces the matchMedia listener (e.g., copy-pasting from the verdict bar chart) would not be caught automatically. The EXP claims 'node test.js still passes' but does not propose adding tests to enforce the two grep-style criteria.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider can add two lightweight assertions to test.js section 9 (Phase A Design Refresh) at integration time, asserting that no hex appears inside emitted pie callouts and that the matchMedia listener no longer appears inside the rendered pie SVG block. These are 2-line additions and they convert grep criteria into regression-resistant tests. Not blocking — the EXP is integratable as-is, but follow-up tests would lock in the gain.

holes_found.description: Callout-line color behavior changes silently. Currently, .callout-line and .pie-callout are NOT in the matchMedia toggle (the toggle only swaps .pie-slice and .legend-swatch fills). So callouts render in 'darkColor' (the dark-theme hex) regardless of theme — a visual inconsistency that anyone who looked closely could spot. After the change, callouts use 'var(--*-solid)' which DOES respond to theme: light theme will draw callouts in the deeper light-theme color (#7a2e2e for Refuted-callout), dark theme in the lighter dark-theme color (#d46a6a). This is arguably a fix (consistent theme response) but it is a visual delta the EXP does not enumerate. For Not Demonstrated (4 wins) and Unfalsifiable (4 wins) — both below the 8% threshold and therefore drawn with callouts — the light-mode callouts will get noticeably darker.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a third bullet to acceptance_criteria.visual: 'Callout-line strokes and outside labels for small slices (fraction < 8% — currently Not Demonstrated and Unfalsifiable) now respond to theme via var(--*-solid). Light-mode callouts shift to the deeper light-theme verdict color; dark-mode callouts shift to the lighter dark-theme color. This is a correction to a current inconsistency where callouts were locked to dark-theme hex regardless of mode.' Confirms the behavior change is intentional.

holes_found.description: Print rendering claim is correct but slightly oversold. EXP says: 'Previously this was implicit (matchMedia returned false during Chromium print emulation); now it is explicit (CSS @media print:root sets the tokens).' In fact the current code's matchMedia listener IS attached to the document, and during Playwright print emulation the page emulates 'prefers-color-scheme: light' (default) so 'mq.matches' is false and slices stay light. So the current print pathway is ALREADY light-themed. The EXP improvement is real (no JS dependency, deterministic at first paint, no race condition), but framing it as a fix to a current bug is a slight overclaim — the current behavior is correct, just JS-dependent.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional polish — adjust the rationale in change_3.rationale to clarify that the change converts JS-dependent print correctness into CSS-dependent print correctness, rather than fixing a current print bug. Both pathways produce the same result today; the EXP makes the dependency on Playwright print-emulation behavior unnecessary.

stronger_arguments.description: The EXP could note that the matchMedia removal also fixes a subtle ARIA / accessibility tree concern: the current pie chart re-paints fill attributes via JS at theme-change time, which may cause screen reader implementations that snapshot the SVG accessibility tree to emit redundant change events. Switching to CSS-driven theme means the accessibility tree is stable across theme changes. This is a small additional gain not currently called out in 'holes_addressed'.
sources: ARIA SVG semantic tree behavior in NVDA/VoiceOver

stronger_arguments.description: The new --label-* tokens are documented in change_1/2/3 but not in any user-facing token reference. If the project ever ships a token reference doc (per the design-system audit pattern in EXP-211-redesign-v2 H6), --label-* should be added with rationale. Worth flagging as a follow-up issue rather than expanding this EXP.
sources: EXP-211-redesign-v2 H6 (test-infrastructure gap, similar follow-up pattern)

conditional_approval_assessment.rubric: DIRECTIVE-003 design rubric, applied to a Phase B EXP. Per HNOTE-PHASE-B-DESIGN-REFRESH-COMMISSION-001: 'no operator approval gate; decider may auto-integrate after curmudgeon clears.'
conditional_approval_assessment.integration_recommendation: PROCEED. Zero ESCALATE / critical / major findings. The three moderate findings (H1 visual contradiction, H2 contrast arithmetic, H3 tap-target overclaim) are documentation-precision fixes that affect the integration-test checklist but do not block the structural change. Two pathways are acceptable: (a) decider applies the three moderate fixes inline at integration time (each is a small text edit to acceptance_criteria.visual or contrast_table — recommended values are in this review's contrast_audit and visual_delta_audit sections), or (b) route the EXP back to analyst for a v2 with the three moderate findings addressed before integration. Pathway (a) is faster and consistent with the conditional-approval rubric; pathway (b) is more conservative if the operator wants the EXP to self-document accurately before merging. Minor findings (H4-H7) can be deferred to a follow-up polish issue.
conditional_approval_assessment.audit_trail: EXP-249 is the first Phase B EXP through DIRECTIVE-003 in this cycle. No prior cycle on this target_id. queue_id 261, pushed_at 2026-04-25T17:27:34.426Z, target_type proposal.

text_fingerprint.note: Proposal review — no user-visible prose to fingerprint. Size is the EXP-249 expansion file.

citations_verified: Token values for --refuted-solid (= var(--semantic-crit) = #7a2e2e light, #d46a6a dark), --stdmodel-solid (#66BB6A light, #43a047 dark), --selfcon-solid (#42A5F5 light, #1e88e5 dark), --misleading-solid (#FFA726 light, #fb8c00 dark), --notdemo-solid (#AB47BC light, #8e24aa dark), --unfalsifiable-solid (#BDBDBD light, #757575 dark) — read directly from generate-html.js lines 496, 497, 646; values match EXP contrast_table inputs.

Current pie chart code at lines 315-432, including VERDICT_COLORS_LIGHT (line 318), VERDICT_COLORS_DARK (line 327), VERDICT_ORDER (line 336), generatePieChart (line 341), legend renderer (lines 401-410), and matchMedia toggle (lines 416-430).

Existing var(--*-solid) consumers at line 548 (.vb-bar.vb-refuted), line 556 (.vl-* family), line 561 (td.v-refuted), line 572 (.vt-* family), line 615 (.ks-status.ks-claimed) — confirms the EXP's claim that the rest of the design system is already token-routed.
test.js (753 lines) has no pie chart internal assertions; legend-count contract uses substring matching that the new emit pattern satisfies. node test.js against current main passes 3452/0.
All 12 contrast_table claims independently recomputed via WCAG 2.1 luminance formula (see contrast_audit.recomputed_table).
HNOTE-PHASE-B-DESIGN-REFRESH-COMMISSION-001 §B1 commission text — verified against monitor/analyst/human-notes.json; matches the EXP's stated scope and out-of-scope.

Queue entry: queue_id 261, target_id EXP-249, target_type proposal, pushed_by decider, pushed_at 2026-04-25T17:27:34.426Z — verified against monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json after fresh git pull.
citations_failed: EXP claim 'the slice itself was already on var(--refuted-solid) per EXP-210' (acceptance_criteria.visual[a]) — incorrect; the pie chart was NOT routed through --refuted-solid by EXP-210. See H1.
EXP claim Misleading-light + dark ink = 12:1 — recomputed 8.96:1. Direction correct, magnitude overstated. See H2.
EXP claim SelfCon-dark = 4.3:1 borderline — recomputed 4.73:1, comfortably above threshold. The actual borderline case is Unfalsifiable-dark at 4.61:1. See H2.
EXP claim 'tap target envelope now satisfies WCAG 2.5.5 AAA (44x44)' (acceptance_criteria.visual[c]) — overstated; legend rows are not interactive, so the criterion does not apply. See H3.
Line-number references: '@media print:root around line 645' — actual line 646. 'line 547 (.vb-bar.vb-refuted...)' — actual line 548. See H4.
citations_unchecked: EXP claim 'SVG fill=var(--name) supported in all browsers post-2017 (Chrome 49+, Firefox 31+, Safari 9.1+)' — accepted on its own report; not independently verified against caniuse.com or browser release notes this run. The codebase already relies on this pattern (line 548 etc.), so the claim is empirically validated by the existing site rendering correctly.
EXP-209, EXP-210, EXP-213 integration status — accepted on the EXP's own report; not re-verified against monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json this run.

HNOTE-PHASE-B-VERDICT-CHIP-DEFINITION-LINK-002 (the click-through HNOTE the legend stride is sized for) — content read briefly; not deeply audited for whether 48px stride is in fact what B+002 requires.

deeper_analysis: The most interesting tension in this proposal is between the conservative scoping discipline (boundary respected, structural preservation immaculate, signature unchanged) and the audit-quality slips (H1 visual contradiction, H2 contrast arithmetic). Those slips suggest the EXP author was rigorous about CODE preservation but less careful about ACCEPTANCE-CRITERIA precision. Since acceptance criteria drive the integration checklist, and the design refresh sequence (Phase A1, then Phase B1/B2/B3/B4) is now a multi-EXP rolling refactor, audit precision matters more than usual — each EXP's acceptance criteria become the input to the next EXP's preservation_audit. If H1 (visual contradiction on Refuted slice) is left in, a future EXP author auditing the pie chart will read 'slice was already on var(--refuted-solid) per EXP-210' and conclude EXP-210 did the routing — which is wrong, and could lead to over-confidence in token coverage. Recommendation to decider: prefer pathway (b) of the conditional approval (route back for v2) ONLY if the operator wants the EXP-249 file itself to be self-accurate as documentation of what landed; pathway (a) (fix at integration) is acceptable but the integration commit message should explicitly note that the contrast_table and visual[a] were corrected at integration so that future audits trust the closed-out EXP-249 file. From an adversarial perspective, none of the holes here would let the dome defender attack the review's credibility — the changes are project-internal site mechanics, not dome-claim adjudication. The 'kernel of truth' lens applies at the proposal level: the proposal is largely correct, and the holes are precision rather than substance. The rubric's 'no new ESCALATE' test passes cleanly.
consider_the_other_side: Defender-of-the-EXP perspective: the contrast_table values may have been computed using the WCAG-G18 simplified formula or a pre-rounded table from a tool like WebAIM, which gives values within ~10% of the canonical formula. That would explain the +/- discrepancies but not the 25% Misleading-light overstatement. More plausibly, the values were estimated and the EXP author intended the table as illustrative rather than canonical. Even on that reading, the framing 'auditable arithmetic, not vibes' is what creates the discrepancy — soften the framing or fix the arithmetic, but don't keep both. Defender on H1 (Refuted slice no shift): possibly the author meant 'the OTHER verdict-encoded surfaces (table cells, bar chart) were already on var(--refuted-solid), so post-EXP-249 the pie chart matches them — net visual unification' which is true. But the literal text says 'the slice itself was already', not 'the slice now matches'. Charitable reading is available but the literal text is wrong. Defender on H3 (tap-target AAA): the author may consider the legend AS A WHOLE (the SVG rect element) interactive in the sense that it has role=img with an aria-label. But role=img is non-interactive; AAA tap-target sizing applies to controls. The defender position is weak here.

trigger_context.queue_reason: Phase B1 pie chart cleanup expansion (HNOTE-PHASE-B-DESIGN-REFRESH-COMMISSION-001 §B1). Awaiting DIRECTIVE-003 design rubric review before integration.
